title: cordylanthus littoralis subsp. platycephalus
intro: Cordylanthus littoralis subsp. platycephalus, also known as the San Diego bird's beak, is a plant species native to the coastal sage scrub habitats of southern California. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and support cardiovascular health. Cordylanthus littoralis subsp. platycephalus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
